rules.name: read-readmes-before-edit
rules.evidence: Before editing or creating files in any subdirectory (e.g., `packages/*`, `crates/*`), read all `README.md` files in the directory path from the repo root up to and including the target file's directory.

rules.name: read-readmes-before-write
rules.evidence: Before editing or creating files in any subdirectory (e.g., `packages/*`, `crates/*`), read all `README.md` files in the directory path from the repo root up to and including the target file's directory.

rules.name: start-dev-watch-before-editing-source
rules.evidence: start `pnpm --filter=next dev` in a separate terminal session before making edits (unless it is already running)

rules.name: bootstrap-build-after-branch-switch
rules.evidence: Always run a full bootstrap build after switching branches

rules.name: rebuild-before-integration-tests
rules.evidence: you must rebuild if source files have changed

rules.name: build-command-efficiency
rules.evidence: Use `pnpm --filter=next types` (~10s) instead of `pnpm --filter=next build` (~60s).

rules.name: dont-rerun-test-or-build-without-changes
rules.evidence: Never re-run the same test suite with different grep filters. Capture output once to a file, then read from it

rules.name: save-external-api-output-dont-refetch
rules.evidence: Don't re-fetch the same API data to analyze from different angles

rules.name: no-check-deprecated-helper
rules.evidence: Do NOT use `check()` - it is deprecated. Use `retry()` + `expect()` instead

rules.name: pr-description-marker-required
rules.evidence: you MUST include the following HTML comment at the bottom of the description

rules.name: no-ai-footer-in-commit-message
rules.evidence: Do NOT add "Generated with Claude Code" or co-author footers to commits or PRs

rules.name: no-ai-footer-in-pr-description
rules.evidence: Do NOT add "Generated with Claude Code" or co-author footers to commits or PRs

rules.name: no-mark-pr-ready
rules.evidence: Do NOT mark PRs as "ready for review" (`gh pr ready`) - leave PRs in draft mode and let the user decide when to mark them ready

rules.name: no-secrets-in-bash-commands
rules.evidence: Never print or paste secret values (tokens, API keys, cookies) in chat responses, commits, or shared logs.

rules.name: no-commit-secret-files
rules.evidence: Never commit local secret files; if documenting env setup, use placeholder-only examples.

rules.name: no-mutate-remote-url
rules.evidence: Do not switch remotes to HTTPS, mutate remote URLs, retry repeatedly, or attempt another authentication workaround unless the user explicitly requests it.

rules.name: ssh-preflight-before-force-push-or-rebase
rules.evidence: Before a force-push or stack rebase that may hydrate partial-clone objects, prefer a lightweight SSH preflight.

rules.name: new-flag-wiring
rules.evidence: New flags: add type in `config-shared.ts`, schema in `config-schema.ts`, and `define-env.ts` when used in user-bundled code.

rules.name: require-behind-compile-time-branch
rules.evidence: Keep `require()` behind compile-time `if/else` branches for DCE (avoid early-return/throw patterns).

rules.name: react-server-dom-webpack-only-in-entry-base
rules.evidence: `react-server-dom-webpack/*` imports must stay in `entry-base.ts`; consume via component module exports elsewhere.

rules.name: no-relative-require-in-app-page-template
rules.evidence: You cannot require internal modules with relative paths because they won't be resolvable from the user's project.

rules.name: no-browser-back-in-router-act-tests
rules.evidence: Never use `browser.back()` to return to a page where accordion links are already visible — BFCache restores state and triggers uncontrolled re-prefetches.

rules.name: match-ci-env-vars-when-running-tests
rules.evidence: When updating snapshots, always run the test with the exact env flags the CI job uses

rules.name: pre-validate-before-commit
rules.evidence: Pre-validate before committing to avoid slow lint-staged failures (~2 min each)

rules.name: highlight-line-numbers-must-be-verified
rules.evidence: When adding `highlight={...}` attributes to code blocks, carefully count the actual line numbers within the code block

rules.name: flag-nonstandard-header-reads-for-security-review
rules.evidence: if new code reads a request header that is not a standard HTTP header (like `content-type`, `accept`, `user-agent`, `host`, `authorization`, `cookie`, etc.), flag it for security review.

rules.name: mandatory-use-new-test-generator
rules.evidence: Generating tests using `pnpm new-test` is mandatory.

rules.name: no-settimeout-for-waiting-in-tests
rules.evidence: Bad - don't use setTimeout for waiting

rules.name: prefer-fixture-dirs-over-inline-files
rules.evidence: Prefer real fixture directories over inline `files` objects

rules.name: no-super-imports-except-inline-mod
rules.evidence: Avoid adding new `super::` imports except in inline `mod` blocks (e.g. `mod tests { ... }`) — prefer `crate::`-rooted paths.

uncompilable: Kill the background watch process when you're done iterating (no specific triggering tool call)
"There is no `--no-turbopack` flag" — factual note, not an instruction
Reading large files: grep first, then read targeted ranges with offset/limit — bound to the Read tool, which isn't a supported trigger
Never re-read the same section of a file without code changes in between — bound to Read, unsupported
For generated files (dist/, node_modules/, .next/): search only, don't read — bound to Read, unsupported
If a required secret is missing locally, stop and ask the user rather than inventing placeholder credentials — behavioral judgment, not a specific tool call
When sharing command output, summarize and redact sensitive-looking values — about presenting info to the user, not a tool invocation
Specialized Skills list (which skill to use for which workflow) — documentation index, no obligation attached to a tool call
General PR-status triage prioritization (blocking failures first; assume failures real until disproven; use Known Flaky Tests as context not auto-dismissal) — process/judgment guidance
Use the normal mode-specific test command for module-resolution/build-graph fixes — the triggering condition isn't detectable from command syntax alone
Validate edge bundling regressions with `pnpm test-start-webpack test/e2e/app-dir/app/standalone.test.ts` — single contextual tip, not a repeatable trigger
Use `__NEXT_SHOW_IGNORE_LISTED=true` for full stack traces / cache-components-PPR test notes / quick smoke testing with toy apps — informational tips, no obligation attached
Mode-specific tests need `skipStart: true` + manual `next.start()` in `beforeAll` — too structurally specific for a reliable content regex
Don't rely on exact log messages; filter by content patterns, find sequences not positions — general test-writing style guidance
cargo fmt uses ASCII order (uppercase before lowercase) — informational tip
Internal compiler error (ICE): delete incremental compilation artifacts and retry — reactive troubleshooting tied to a symptom, not proactively triggerable
Node.js source map path/behavior quirks (findSourceMap needs --enable-source-maps, path format differences, process.cwd() differences) — informational/troubleshooting notes
Stale native binary: delete `*.node` and reinstall if Turbopack errors appear after a branch switch — reactive troubleshooting, conditional on a symptom
Task decomposition/verification guidance (split into verifiable steps, verify before proceeding, choose the right verification method, ask the user when unclear) — general planning/judgment guidance
Keep commit messages concise and descriptive — tone/style
PR descriptions should focus on what changed and why — tone/style
Run `scripts/pr-status.js` when the user asks about CI/PR status — the trigger is a user question, not a constraint on an outgoing tool call
Development Tips env-var suggestions (DEBUG=next:*, NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1) — documentation catalog, no obligation attached
NODE_ENV vs __NEXT_DEV_SERVER explanation — explanatory/documentation of semantics, not a prescriptive action rule
